headline: Radiation and Wombats Advance as Playoff Field Narrows to Final Four
subheadline: Dominant semifinal performances set the stage for what promises to be a compelling championship showdown in MillerLite® BeerLeagueBaseball.
body:
The postseason, as it so often does, has a way of separating the merely good from the genuinely excellent. And in Week 22 of MillerLite® BeerLeagueBaseball, we witnessed that separation with striking clarity.

**Hiroshima Radiation** delivered perhaps the most complete playoff performance we've seen this season, dismantling the **Albuquerque Bombers** by a 7.0–2.0 margin that, frankly, doesn't fully capture the one-sidedness of the affair. The Bombers, who entered the postseason with legitimate championship aspirations, managed just two category wins against a Radiation squad that has quietly built one of the deepest rosters in the league. This is a team constructed with the kind of patient, methodical approach that baseball people have long admired—acquiring complementary pieces rather than chasing headlines, valuing consistency over flash.

Equally impressive was the **Canberra Wombats'** handling of the **Central Wisconsin Curd Fryers**, a 7.0–3.0 victory that extended what has been a remarkable run of form. The Wombats won this matchup across nearly every meaningful category, and their roster construction—balanced between power and speed, between established performers and emerging talents—speaks to front office work that deserves recognition regardless of how the championship round unfolds.

In the consolation bracket, **Riverside Roughnecks** edged **Hung Jung** in a tightly contested 6.0–4.0 affair, the kind of competitive match that reminds us how thin the margins can be between playoff teams and those playing out the string. **Portage County Heartbreakers** similarly dispatched **Buttville Skidmarks** by a 6.0–3.0 count, salvaging what has been a season of unfulfilled potential.

The standings now reset as we approach championship week—a clean slate where regular season dominance means nothing and single-week performance means everything. **Hiroshima Radiation** and the **Canberra Wombats** will meet with the title on the line, and the analytical case can be made for either side. Both have demonstrated the roster depth necessary to weather the unpredictability that defines fantasy playoffs.

What strikes me most, having observed this league throughout the season, is how both finalists arrived at this moment through sustainable team-building rather than lucky breaks. Championships are won in March, the old saying goes, but they're built in the preceding months through hundreds of small decisions.

Next week, one team hoists the trophy. The other goes home wondering what might have been. That's October baseball—even in the beer league variety.
